xposts.title: Neurons simulate a self-aware person
xposts.excerpt: neurons are not self-aware either. Instead, our neurons *simulate* a person, which experiences itself as self-aware. Can LLMs simulate a self-aware person too, or are they just simulating the simulation of a person?

xposts.title: Consciousness simulates a dissatisfied agent
xposts.excerpt: Pain and suffering are not a feature of the world. They are generated by consciousness, as part of its simulation of a dissatisfied agent. But your consciousness can gain full agency over what it generates.

xposts.title: Consciousness is not a specific story or narrative
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness is not necessarily tied to any particular contents beyond self reflexivity (second order perception) and nowness... Consciousness is not a specific story or narrative.

xposts.title: Consciousness is itself a dream
xposts.excerpt: I don’t think we can, consciousness is itself a dream

xposts.title: Consciousness is a dream state
xposts.excerpt: can't you see that consciousness is a dream state

xposts.title: Mental model imbued with consciousness via dream
xposts.excerpt: You can imbibe a mental model of a spring with consciousness (dream to be a spring), so you will subjectively experience the interface between spring model and environment model

terms.title: simulation
terms.definition: The brain/neural substrate does not host awareness directly but runs a simulation of a person that experiences itself as self-aware; consciousness generates simulations including of a dissatisfied agent and of boundaries between observer and observed.

terms.title: dream
terms.definition: Consciousness is itself a dream state; without perceptual constraints the mind spins into dreams, and one can dream to be a model or spring, experiencing the interface subjectively.
